# Python verifier — cross-implementation conformance for v2.4 learn-and-lock

Independently verifies the Ed25519 signatures on Go-emitted golden vectors,
proving byte-identical JCS canonicalization between the Go and Python
implementations.

## Usage

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt
python3 verify.py ../../internal/contract/testdata/golden/
```

Output: `OK <fixture>` per fixture or `FAIL <fixture>: <reason>`.
Exits 0 on full success, 1 on any failure.

## Dependency pinning

`requirements.in` is the human-editable source manifest with version
bounds and rationale comments. `requirements.txt` is the autogenerated
lockfile with `==`-pinned versions and `--hash=sha256:...` lines for
every wheel and sdist; this is the file `pip install --require-hashes`
consumes.

To refresh the lock after editing `requirements.in`:

```bash
python3 -m venv /tmp/pip-compile-venv
/tmp/pip-compile-venv/bin/pip install pip-tools
/tmp/pip-compile-venv/bin/pip-compile \
    --generate-hashes \
    --strip-extras \
    --output-file=requirements.txt \
    requirements.in
```

Loose `>=` ranges in `requirements.txt` are forbidden. OSV-Scanner has
historically over-reported on range pins, leaking false-positive
Scorecard alerts onto main. The lockfile pattern eliminates that class
of noise and gives reproducible installs across developer machines and
CI. See `CONTRIBUTING.md` Python dependencies section for the full
rule.

Dependabot watches the `pip` ecosystem at this directory and at
`.github/`; it auto-opens PRs to bump pinned versions when a new
advisory drops.

## CI integration

The Go-side shim test `internal/contract/python_roundtrip_test.go` runs this
verifier when `python3` is available and is skipped under `go test -short`.
